title: The Wright Brothers' First Flight
description: A photograph of Orville Wright piloting the Wright Flyer at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina, marking the first powered, controlled, and sustained flight in history.

title: Gibson Girl Illustrations
description: Charles Dana Gibson's illustrations of the 'Gibson Girl' became iconic representations of the independent and fashionable women of the early 20th century.

title: San Francisco Earthquake and Fire
description: Photographs documenting the devastation caused by the earthquake and subsequent fires in San Francisco, including images of collapsed buildings and the widespread destruction.

title: Albert Einstein's Graduation Photo
description: A young Albert Einstein's graduation photo from the Polytechnic Institute in Zurich, Switzerland, where he studied physics and mathematics.

title: Theodore Roosevelt on Horseback
description: A photograph of President Theodore Roosevelt on horseback during one of his many outdoor adventures, reflecting his love for the outdoors.

title: The Great Auto Race from New York to Paris
description: A photograph capturing the start of the New York to Paris automobile race, a historic event in the early days of the automobile industry.

title: First Ford Model T
description: A picture of the first Ford Model T car, which revolutionized the automobile industry by making cars more affordable to the general public.

title: The Breaker Boys
description: Breaker boys: child workers who broke down coal at a mine in South Pittston, Pennsylvania, United States in the early 20th century

title: Pablo Picasso's 'La Vie'
description: While not a photograph, this painting by Picasso is a notable artwork from the early 1900s, reflecting the artist's Blue Period.

title: Hindenburg Zeppelin Construction
description: An image from the construction of the Zeppelin LZ 3, an early airship that paved the way for future advancements in aviation.

title: The Sinking of the RMS Titanic
description: A dramatic photograph of the RMS Titanic sinking into the icy waters of the North Atlantic Ocean after hitting an iceberg on April 15, 1912. This tragic event is one of the most famous maritime disasters in history.

title: Charlie Chaplin's 'The Tramp' Character
description: An iconic image of silent film star Charlie Chaplin in his signature character 'The Tramp.' Chaplin's portrayal of 'The Tramp' became one of the most beloved and enduring characters in film history.

title: Soldiers During World War I
description: A poignant photograph of soldiers in the trenches during World War I. This image symbolizes the hardships and sacrifices made by those who served in the Great War.

title: The Women's Suffrage Movement
description: Photographs capturing the women's suffrage movement, including suffragists advocating for women's right to vote. This movement ultimately led to the ratification of the 19th Amendment to the U.S. Constitution in 1920.

title: Einstein's Theory of General Relativity
description: Photographs documenting the solar eclipse of 1919, during which Sir Arthur Eddington conducted experiments that confirmed Albert Einstein's theory of general relativity. This was a groundbreaking moment in the history of physics.

title: Charles Lindbergh’s 1927 transatlantic flight
description: Charles Lindbergh completed the first solo, nonstop transatlantic flight from New York to Paris in 33.5 hours, covering 3,610 miles in his monoplane the Spirit of St. Louis, making him an international hero and boosting commercial aviation.

title: The Susan B. Anthony Amendment
description: Kentucky Governor Edwin P. Morrow signs the bill ratifying the Nineteenth Amendment (the 'Susan B. Anthony Amendment'), giving women the right to vote, surrounded by members of the Kentucky Equal Rights Association, on January 6, 1920.

title: A Sea of Hats
description: A crowd of men gather in New York's financial district, trying to get a glimpse of the aftermath of the Wall Street bombing.

title: Lenin Addresses Red Army Soldiers
description: Vladimir Lenin addresses Red Army soldiers during the Polish-Soviet War at Sverdlov Square in Moscow, May 5, 1920, with Leon Trotsky and Lev Kamenev visible on the platform.

title: March on Rome
description: Benito Mussolini and Fascist Blackshirts during the March on Rome in 1922.

title: The Holocaust
description: The Holocaust is without doubt the worst atrocity in history, and resulted in the tragic loss of approximately 11 million people. 6 million of the victims were Jewish, and other groups targeted included homosexuals, Jehovah's Witnesses, disabled people and gypsies.The above photo shows the deplorable conditions the prisoners were forced to live in. May we never forget this abhorrent massacre of innocent lives.

title: The Christmas Truce
description: An unofficial ceasefire on the Western Front in December 1914 during WWI allowed soldiers to meet in no man's land, exchanging food and souvenirs. Many played football together on Christmas Day, showing humanity amid war's tragedy.

title: "I have a dream"
description: Martin Luther King, Jr. led the 1963 March on Washington, delivering his iconic 'I Have a Dream' speech. He advanced civil rights through nonviolent disobedience rooted in Christian beliefs.

title: The Great Depression
description: The women pictured above were arrested for stealing bread for their families during the Great Depression.

title: The Wright Brothers
description: Orville and Wilbur Wright were responsible for creating the world’s first successful airplane. The first flight took place on 17th December 1903. While the plane only remained in the air for 12 seconds, it was a historic breakthrough that would define future aviation.

title: Migrant Mother
description: The image depicts a mother anxiously gazing into the distance, with an infant in her lap and two older children huddling close by. The photo captures the plight of migrant farm workers who arrived in California en masse looking for employment during the Great Depression in the 1930s.

title: Dandi march
description: Mahatma Gandhi's pivotal 240-mile nonviolent Salt March in 1930 challenged Britain's salt monopoly and sparked India's mass Civil Disobedience Movement, uniting Indians against British rule through symbolic protest.

title: Rise of the Nazis
description: The end of the First World War marked the beginning of a period of political and economic instability in Germany. As a result of this instability, many small, extremist political groups appeared. With the collapse of democracy, one such party, the NSDAP, or Nazi Party, rose to power in Germany.

title: The Steerage
description: Alfred Stieglitz's iconic 'The Steerage' exemplifies straight photography, offering a truthful and multi-layered viewpoint through abstraction and the interplay of shapes in the composition.

title: The Falling Man
description: The man in this image is believed to be Jonathon Briley, who, like a number of people stuck on the burning Twin Towers on September 11, 2001, choosed to jump to his death from the tower. It was an image that immortalised the desperation of the situation for those trapped at the top of the Twin Towers after the attack. They were faced with a horrible dilemma, where jumping was the lesser of the two evils.

title: The Holocaust
description: The Holocaust is without doubt the worst atrocity in history, and resulted in the tragic loss of approximately 11 million people. 6 million of the victims were Jewish, and other groups targeted included homosexuals, Jehovah's Witnesses, disabled people and gypsies.The above photo shows the deplorable conditions the prisoners were forced to live in. May we never forget this abhorrent massacre of innocent lives.

title: The Christmas Truce
description: The above photograph highlights the tragedy of war. The soldiers mostly had no quarrel with the men they were fighting against. There was an unofficial ceasefire on the Western Front in Christmas 1914, during the First World War. On Christmas Eve and Christmas Day, many soldiers, on both sides, would venture onto “no man’s land”, where they would chat and exchange food and souvenirs. Plenty of football was also played on Christmas Day between troops from opposing armies, as the image above proves.

title: "I have a dream"
description: Martin Luther King, Jr. led the 1963 March on Washington, delivering his iconic 'I Have a Dream' speech. He advanced civil rights through nonviolent disobedience rooted in Christian beliefs.

title: The Great Depression
description: The women pictured above were arrested for stealing bread for their families during the Great Depression.

title: The Wright Brothers
description: Orville and Wilbur Wright were responsible for creating the world’s first successful airplane. The first flight took place on 17th December 1903. While the plane only remained in the air for 12 seconds, it was a historic breakthrough that would define future aviation.

title: V-J Day Kiss in Times Square
description: A Navy sailor kisses a woman in Times Square on August 14, 1945, celebrating Victory over Japan Day. Published in Life magazine's 'Victory Celebrations,' it became an iconic symbol of war's end.

title: The Aftermath
description: This image shows Hiroshima's devastation after the atomic bombing in August 1945, with buildings reduced to rubble and smoke rising. The bombing caused immense loss of life and lasting health effects.

title: Founding of the United Nations
description: The San Francisco Conference (April-June 1945) brought 50 Allied nations' delegates to draft the UN Charter, promoting international cooperation and preventing future conflicts after WWII.

title: Tryst with Destiny
description: Jawaharlal Nehru's iconic 'Tryst with Destiny' speech on August 14, 1947, articulated India's aspirations upon independence, emphasizing unity, social justice, and progress as a sovereign state.

title: The Last Jew in Vinnitsa
description: This haunting photograph captures the final moments of a Jewish man in Vinnitsa, Ukraine, in 1941, during the Holocaust.

title: Gandhi and his Chakra
description: Margaret Bourke-White, LIFE's first female photographer, learned to use a spinning wheel to photograph Gandhi's most defining portrait in 1946, capturing his most enduring image.

title: Tank Man
description: An unidentified Chinese man stands before tanks on June 5, 1989, after military suppression of Tiananmen Square protests. Captured by photographers including Stuart Franklin, it symbolizes resistance to oppression.

title: The Terror of War
description: Nick Ut's Pulitzer Prize-winning 1972 image shows Vietnamese children fleeing a napalm attack. Nine-year-old Kim Phuc appears at center. Ut rushed them to hospital, saving lives while capturing war's horrors.

title: The Steerage
description: Alfred Stieglitz's iconic 'The Steerage' exemplifies straight photography, offering a truthful and multi-layered viewpoint through abstraction and the interplay of shapes in the composition.

title: The Falling Man
description: The man in this image is believed to be Jonathon Briley, who, like a number of people stuck on the burning Twin Towers on September 11, 2001, choosed to jump to his death from the tower. It was an image that immortalised the desperation of the situation for those trapped at the top of the Twin Towers after the attack. They were faced with a horrible dilemma, where jumping was the lesser of the two evils.

title: The Holocaust
description: The Holocaust is without doubt the worst atrocity in history, and resulted in the tragic loss of approximately 11 million people. 6 million of the victims were Jewish, and other groups targeted included homosexuals, Jehovah's Witnesses, disabled people and gypsies.The above photo shows the deplorable conditions the prisoners were forced to live in. May we never forget this abhorrent massacre of innocent lives.

title: The Christmas Truce
description: The above photograph highlights the tragedy of war. The soldiers mostly had no quarrel with the men they were fighting against. There was an unofficial ceasefire on the Western Front in Christmas 1914, during the First World War. On Christmas Eve and Christmas Day, many soldiers, on both sides, would venture onto “no man’s land”, where they would chat and exchange food and souvenirs. Plenty of football was also played on Christmas Day between troops from opposing armies, as the image above proves.

title: "I have a dream"
description: Martin Luther King, Jr. helped to organise the 1963 March on Washington, where he delivered the famous “I Have a Dream” speech, establishing himself as one of the greatest orators in history. He was pivotal in the advancement of civil rights using non-violent civil disobedience, which were based on his Christian beliefs.

title: The Great Depression
description: The women pictured above were arrested for stealing bread for their families during the Great Depression.

title: The Wright Brothers
description: Orville and Wilbur Wright were responsible for creating the world’s first successful airplane. The first flight took place on 17th December 1903. While the plane only remained in the air for 12 seconds, it was a historic breakthrough that would define future aviation.

title: 'I Have A Dream'
description: Martin Luther King Jr. gave his famous 'I Have A Dream' speech during the march. This speech is widely regarded as one of the greatest speeches in American history and was a defining moment in the civil rights movement.

title: A Legend in the Ring
description: Muhammad Ali knocks out Sonny Liston after a one-minute-long championship match in Lewiston, Maine on May 25, 1965. Just seconds after the knockout, referee Joe Walcott holds Ali back. Ali's victory was a significant moment in sports history, showcasing his skill and charisma, and solidifying his status as one of the greatest boxers of all time.

title: One Giant Leap for Mankind
description: On July 20, 1969, following the Space Race that had pitted the world's superpowers against each other for more than a decade, the U.S. became the first and only country to put a person on the Moon. Astronauts Neil Armstrong and Edward 'Buzz' Aldrin (pictured) walked the surface of the Moon for two minutes and 34 seconds — and in that brief time, made history.

title: First American in Space
description: Rear Admiral Alan Bartlett 'Al' Shepard Jr. right before takeoff in May 1961. Shepard became the first American, and the second person ever, to travel into space. He was also the first to manually control a spacecraft. Shepard's historic flight lasted just 15 minutes, but it was a crucial step in the United States' efforts to catch up with the Soviet Union in the Space Race.

title: The Crash of Apollo 13
description: Just two days into its cardinal journey, the Apollo 13 spacecraft had to make an emergency landing in the South Pacific near Samoa. Millions of Americans watched as the lives of the three crew members hung in the balance for days on end.

title: 'The Godfather' wins at the 47th Academy Awards.
description: The Godfather Part II is touted as one of the only films to escape the dismal trope of washed-up sequels, with many considering it vastly superior to its predecessor. The flick took home seven Oscars at the Dorothy Chandler Pavilion in April 1975.

title: Arthur Wins Wimbledon
description: This image captures the moment when Arthur Ashe won the men's singles title at the 1968 Wimbledon Championships. Ashe became the first African American to win the tournament, breaking racial barriers in tennis.

title: Steve Wozniak and Steve Jobs found Apple.
description: In the garage of a Los Altos ranch home, the most impactful corporation in technology was conceived. The spring of 1976 brought Steve Wozniak and Steve Jobs to Jobs's childhood home to brainstorm a computer project that would snowball into the world's most valuable brand.

title: Gandhi and his Chakra
description: April 1981 saw the launch and successful return of the world’s first reusable spacecraft, the Space Shuttle Columbia. The Space Shuttle was designed to launch like a rocket, orbit like a spacecraft and land like a plane, making transport to and from Earth more efficient and affordable.

title: The first version of Windows released in 1985
description: When the first version of Microsoft’s now ubiquitous operating system, Windows, was released on 20 November 1985, it was met with an almost universal ‘meh.’ It was clunky and confusing, especially when compared with the more user-friendly graphical user interface developed by Apple for the Macintosh. But Bill Gates, the (then) young president of Microsoft, seen here in March of the same year, had the foresight to license his OS to other computer manufacturers, meaning it was used in office computers across the nation. By version 3.0, Windows was more user-friendly, transforming Microsoft into the trillion-dollar company it is today.

title: Greed is Good
description: The movie Wall Street was released on 11 December 1987 and quickly became a box office success around the world. Michael Douglas memorably played the central character of Gordon Gecko, a cigar smoking financial trader whose mantra 'Greed is Good' came to represent the excesses of the Eighties.

title: The Berlin Wall is brought down
description: Although it happened on the other side of the world, the fall of the Berlin Wall on 9 November 1989 and the subsequent breakup of the Soviet Union was very much the result of American foreign policy throughout the 1980s.

title: John Lennon Murdered in 1980
description: Shockwaves reverberated around the world on 8 December 1980 when John Lennon was brutally shot outside his apartment building in New York City. The former Beatle was returning to the Dakota Building with his wife Yoko Ono when his murderer stepped out of the shadows and fired five shots, hitting Lennon four times in the back and shoulder.

title: The Hubble Space Telescope is deployed in 1990
description: Captured at Cape Canaveral, Florida, in April 1990, this cinematic photo shows Space Shuttle Discovery astronauts leaving HQ on the way to launch pad 39-B at Kennedy Space Center.

title: The USA host the FIFA World Cup
description: When soccer fans recall the World Cup 1994, a few things probably spring to mind. From Diana Ross missing a penalty at the opening ceremony to the scorching hot weather and Diego Maradona testing positive for banned substances, it wasn't a tournament short of talking points.

title: Friends released in 1994
description: Ross, Joey, Chandler, Phoebe, Rachel, and Monica are perhaps the most recognizable half a dozen character names on the planet. Television sitcom Friends first aired in 1994 and quickly became an international phenomenon.

title: Pixar's Toy Story changes animation forever
description: 'To infinity and beyond' was the immortal line uttered by the space ranger Buzz Lightyear in Pixar's classic animated movie Toy Story. In many ways, though, it's a piece of dialogue that reflects the extent to which this influential film would transform moviemaking forever.

title: Titanic wins 11 Academy Awards
description: The film Titanic, directed by James Cameron, won 11 Academy Awards in 1998, including Best Picture. The movie, which starred Leonardo DiCaprio and Kate Winslet, became a cultural phenomenon and remains one of the highest-grossing films of all time.
